role_title: Member of Technical Staff (AI Policy and Strategic Initiatives)
secondary_archetype: BizOps / Strategy & Ops with engineering focus (AI policy, legal, corporate affairs)

seniority.level: Early-career to mid-level IC (new grads to experienced engineers both welcome)
level_signals: minimum 2-year commitment requested for candidates planning law school or PhD
experienced engineers at top tech companies also welcome
reports to VP Global Affairs & Deputy CTO

must_haves.raw_text_from_jd: Evidence of exceptional ability and 99th-percentile work ethic.
Strong technical skills at or above Perplexity's hiring bar for software engineers.
Fluency with frontier intelligence tools (such as Perplexity Computer).
High intrinsically-enforced standards for the craft and quality of your work.
Strong communication and writing skills, ideally demonstrated via samples of past written work product.
Curiosity and excitement about new and unfamiliar fields; eagerness to redefine your job every 3-6 months.
Ability to quickly dive deep in any given field and develop mastery rivaling that of domain experts.
Unimpeachable judgment, integrity, and discretion to be entrusted with the company's most important matters.
A service-oriented attitude that continually questions how company systems and processes might better serve stakeholders.
We ask for a minimum 2-year commitment (i.e., you would apply for the entering class of 2028 or later).

nice_to_haves.raw_text_from_jd: Exceptionally talented early-career engineers (new grads welcome) who are (1) well-read in law, tech policy, or economics, and (2) wish to grow in both technical and non-technical disciplines.
Experienced engineers at top tech companies who wish to develop policy, business, and operations chops.
Lawyers or economists (new JD or PhD grads welcome) who've kept their engineering skills sharp and are excited to reimagine their fields in the age of AI.

scope.surface: AI policy, law, operations, corporate affairs, and strategic technology initiatives across all of Perplexity
scope.business_model: frontier AI consumer and enterprise search/assistant product (B2C and B2B)
primary_metrics_inferred: Quality of regulatory filings and policy contributions
Efficiency of compliance and privacy systems
Volume and quality of patents filed
Operational effectiveness of implemented software solutions
core_responsibilities: Harness AI agents to research high-stakes policy and regulatory issues pertaining to Perplexity.
Build automated platforms for harvesting, prioritizing, and patenting Perplexity's inventions, in collaboration with our AI researchers and intellectual property attorneys.
Work hands-on with our Legal team and outside counsel in support of high-tech litigation matters in U.S. federal court.
Engineer privacy and compliance systems that efficiently scale with our growing portfolio of frontier AI products (such as Comet and Computer).
Contribute to regulatory filings and responses that require impeccable command of AI technology, Perplexity's own systems, and the broader policy landscape.
Embed with our People team to implement first-party software solutions for recruiting, labor compliance, and performance management.
Participate in meetings with Capitol Hill, executive branch officials, international partners, and civil society as a subject-matter expert on both AI technology and policy.

green_flags: Highly unusual and interdisciplinary role for engineers interested in law, policy, and operations
Direct reporting line to VP Global Affairs & Deputy CTO — high visibility
Explicitly welcoming to new grads across multiple profiles (engineering, law, economics)
Mentorship explicitly promised ('a bench of mentors who will push you to learn and grow')
Role spans frontier AI issues including regulatory, litigation, privacy, and IP — broad learning opportunity
red_flags: No location or remote/hybrid policy stated anywhere in the JD
Extremely broad scope ('no two days will be the same') may indicate role is loosely defined or subject to significant scope creep
Minimum 2-year commitment constraint may conflict with candidates planning near-term graduate school
Work auth and visa sponsorship entirely unstated

fields_inferred_not_stated: seniority.level (inferred from JD text describing new grads through experienced engineers)
scope.primary_metrics_inferred (inferred from responsibilities; not in JD)
ambiguities: Role title is 'Member of Technical Staff' but the scope is heavily non-engineering (policy, legal, corporate affairs). Archetype is difficult to classify; 'Technical Program Manager' is the closest fit but does not fully capture the role. 'Other' was considered but TPM better reflects the cross-functional coordination and technical rigor.

Ideal candidate profiles are described as non-exhaustive examples rather than firm requirements, making it difficult to classify must-haves vs nice-to-haves for candidate profiles. Law/economics backgrounds are framed as optional profiles rather than requirements.
2-year commitment clause is directed only at candidates planning to attend law school or PhD programs, but is worded ambiguously as a general requirement.
validation_issues: must_have two_year_commitment: raw_text_from_jd not found in JD even after bullet/whitespace normalization
